hypothesis: Applying NN-BAX to reaction pathways governed by density functional theory rather than the Lennard-Jones potential will reveal a non-monotonic relationship between the computational cost savings and the dimensionality of the atomic system, such that systems with intermediate dimensionality (20–60 atoms) achieve the greatest wall-clock speedup because the overhead of fine-tuning EquiformerV2 per BAX iteration scales more favorably than DFT cost in this regime, whereas very high-dimensional systems (>100 atoms) will see diminishing returns as the number of BAX iterations required for MAEi convergence grows faster than the reduction in DFT function evaluations.
